releases.name: v2.12.0
releases.body:
## [2.12.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.11.1...v2.12.0) (2026-06-19)


### Features

* **config:** generate config docs + schema from a single source ([#2597](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2597) Phase 1) ([#2604](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2604)) ([f56d1c6](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/f56d1c67230b7173f0d4f3b0a070ec183bf47748))
* **docs:** interactive config explorer ([#2597](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2597) Phase 2) ([#2606](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2606)) ([a68875a](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/a68875a3145b038125494b3a0421e364f7084e0d))
* **download:** save options, policy-block notification, device-switch guard ([#2652](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2652)) ([9ebd4ba](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/9ebd4ba172e3158abdae723c2663dc99ca292e43))
* **mqtt:** add configurable media topic names and mute/unmute commands ([#2608](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2608)) ([777d5f4](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/777d5f4850e16ee846a464b67d585a176057ae31))
* **window:** add minimizeOnClose option ([#2623](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2623)) ([70efed0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/70efed0f3d440fedf56d2cb12d887945f46ea7d8))


### Bug Fixes

* **auth:** don't divert main-frame navigations through about:blank handler ([#2605](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2605)) ([0853c9d](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/0853c9d80ca875b83ec2582968a9d22b0362583f)), closes [#2591](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2591)
* **auth:** gate in-app reauth recovery behind reauthRecovery.enabled ([#2633](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2633)) ([55b185e](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/55b185e6375d486049592fd63656949773a64789))
* **auth:** gate worker UPR recovery behind reauthRecovery opt-in ([#2629](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2629)) ([9fd1873](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/9fd187369abccbef8c27b5e171eb3c0454520a7d))
* **auth:** intercept login popups to trigger in-app recovery ([#2622](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2622)) ([598f9a6](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/598f9a6cb8bd6c40697c5dafd34b174d0a5a4fc0))
* **ci:** extract Electron binary with system unzip (e2e) ([#2594](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2594)) ([a431a2d](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/a431a2dad6382b61a60cba119988d7d6ec222e18))
* **ci:** use honest release-please PR header ([#2648](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2648)) ([92b6cad](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/92b6caded34f361d9b67c6e7ab33604c18236aa3))
* **deps:** bump docs-site deps to patched versions (Dependabot) ([#2674](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2674)) ([89aa8c1](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/89aa8c131a54ad6586b16356cadd46dd01831f99))
* **navigation:** keep back/forward working after Teams top-bar restructure ([#2672](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2672)) ([189cc9f](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/189cc9f5f28327b3fbd7de97dc87643d7d79f773))
* **net:** add timeout to connectivity probes to prevent hang after resume ([#2635](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2635)) ([944770c](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/944770c2055f6b25052212b306055b4bd0510197))
* **theme:** stop setting followOsTheme to prevent GraphQL rejection ([#2673](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2673)) ([19afd0c](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/19afd0cd1cf692f59295ec7e28839cb9589c8f4c))

### Code Improvements

### Documentation

* **install:** document the beta apt/rpm channel for pre-releases ([#2638](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2638)) ([d53d0f3](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/d53d0f3f5de0dcc53b2e6ef4ec33e4fbd89f9343)), closes [#2637](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2637)
* move repository install instructions to the dedicated webpage ([#2619](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2619)) ([a3543ae](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/a3543ae810d1abf4d96d055701bf6eaf3def0d70))
* **research:** documentation, contributing, and config UX ([#2598](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2598)) ([648aa2c](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/648aa2ce68d2f398c27e257e669d1fdd10528c22))
* **research:** FIDO2 touch-prompt feasibility note ([#2631](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2631)) ([#2634](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2634)) ([8b6eacb](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/8b6eacbea71a045eed6b18736de80e640380f5ed))
* **research:** smartcard/NSS PIN dialog feasibility for client-cert auth ([#2641](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2641)) ([0cbd31a](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/0cbd31a6b788e6897fa122d4c4db233242f7b9da)), closes [#2639](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2639)
* **roadmap:** note in-review download, macOS, and device-switch work ([#2655](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2655)) ([966e500](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/966e500253a4cce1081c355f37873ce654300625))
* **troubleshooting:** document Google sign-in user-agent workaround ([#2670](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2670)) ([5fabd75](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/5fabd75773f26bd5bd2b5a7bf9f344885e9d2fe6))


### CI/CD

* **snap:** pin arm64 snapcraft below 9.0.0 to fix the snap build ([#2649](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2649)) ([df1115b](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/df1115b836b303024d7237305356889173d91104))

releases.name: v2.11.0
releases.body:
## [2.11.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.10.0...v2.11.0) (2026-05-27)

**NOTE: Superseded by https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/releases/tag/v2.11.1 a build failure meant 2.11.0 didn't get any artifacts.**

teams-for-linux 2.11.0 is out with new sticker support and improved Home Assistant integration.

## Highlights
- Custom stickers MVP: add your own stickers to the chat panel (#2550). opt-in
- MQTT + Home Assistant: new microphone (#2497), camera (#2582) and incoming-call (#2572) topics, with Home Assistant auto-discovery so entities are created for you
- Wayland screen sharing fixed: getDisplayMedia now routes through the in-app picker (#2534). Please report if you got any issues after the upgrade.
- WM_CLASS / --class fix: custom window class reaches both X11 and Wayland again, fixing the v2.10.0 regression (#2569)
- Diagnostics cleanup: quieter pre-login logs, full PII sanitising of renderer logs, and a crisper macOS dock icon (#2560). We get a lot of noise in the logs from Microsoft side.
- Electron bumped to 41.7.1 (#2583)

> ## Heads-up
> followSystemTheme now defaults to true, so the app follows your system light/dark theme out of the box (#2566), and six long-deprecated config keys were removed (#2567), so check your config.json if you used old keys.

### Features

* **#2476:** custom stickers MVP ([#2550](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2550)) ([0c31282](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/0c31282151255d89a1f62e9652a782ac5c80c6c4))
* **config:** default followSystemTheme to true ([#2566](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2566)) ([21e927e](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/21e927e6464762b70ffe014bb809fa5bbacb7d5e))
* **mqtt:** emit camera-state-changed from renderer for MQTT publishing ([#2582](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2582)) ([182d559](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/182d559de29104af74cc3a66d8d62a41b187ac97))
* **mqtt:** publish incoming-call topic for ring/invite events ([#2572](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2572)) ([d7cdd06](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/d7cdd06092dc2fd0345a2e1fdb191eeb5e821eb6))
* **mqtt:** publish microphone state to {topicPrefix}/microphone ([#2497](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2497)) ([e8fe6f3](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/e8fe6f3104d24a14e46d87bb792cfbeab76cc942))


### Bug Fixes

* **#2383:** also setDesktopName so --class reaches X11 + Wayland ([#2569](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2569)) ([a048527](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/a048527bde6f5529ee35ec2f04209e1e0c4f4d75))
* **#2534:** route getDisplayMedia through in-app picker on Wayland ([f38f28e](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/f38f28ef81d9c33dd4cbf4d838f9ec940205d311)), closes [#2534](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2534)
* **deps:** bump qs and ws in docs-site to resolve security alerts ([#2573](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2573)) ([84aefe8](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/84aefe84e094bbce8a3124650f23d7f77f7ba5bf))
* **logging:** down-level pre-login auth-noise from renderer ([#2564](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2564)) ([8de3b2d](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/8de3b2d6a1a4842882dca6c99ec33928ad2309ee)), closes [#2560](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2560)
* **logging:** run renderer log fields through full PII sanitizer ([#2562](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2562)) ([bff6d22](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/bff6d22ace2a37195fb3a98ccf5a88d6f2efb98e)), closes [#2560](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2560)
* **macos:** use 256x256 source for the dock icon ([#2563](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2563)) ([8f96c66](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/8f96c6684445c541241de3ad6dd411736af691f8)), closes [#2560](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2560)
* **preload:** serialize object-shaped unhandled rejections ([#2561](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2561)) ([6720768](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/6720768f1ac57c766eeda8595a177f8b0838259e)), closes [#2560](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2560)


### Code Improvements

* **config:** remove six deprecated config keys ([#2567](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2567)) ([4710fb5](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/4710fb53436b32b178301e3d2108fc2e66ea0c49))


### Documentation

* add vylen linux install/remove instruction ([#2559](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2559)) ([2f6c976](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/2f6c97661d1f0b193de1fc425940c8de5b5656b5))
* documentation cleanup and HA discovery docs ([#2575](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2575)) ([6eca43c](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/6eca43cfeca0dddf16062340641f6499aab4afdf))
* update camera topic and MQTT status to shipped ([#2585](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2585)) ([b564e87](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/b564e875a12567934b21b22cd55d2889a6638c55))


### Maintenance

* **deps-dev:** bump electron from 41.6.1 to 41.7.1 ([#2583](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2583)) ([79e9540](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/79e9540bf9dd119b37d7412071a88fee072b5f8a))
* **deps-dev:** bump qs from 6.14.2 to 6.15.2 ([#2568](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2568)) ([a5c0e89](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/a5c0e89fb286cf9d5c4c0a98afadca93ebb8ea19))
* **deps-dev:** bump tmp from 0.2.5 to 0.2.6 ([#2584](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2584)) ([0f3c5ce](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/0f3c5ce4c1c9293d854f0e66044db493fed59c3a))
* **deps:** bump @homebridge/dbus-native in the minor-and-patch group ([#2574](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2574)) ([345dc5e](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/345dc5e752447799bc26a20bf7bfaf121149396e))

releases.name: v2.10.0
releases.body:
## [2.10.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.9.0...v2.10.0) (2026-05-19)

2.10.0 lands **FIDO2 hardware security keys**, the **multi-account Manage dialog**, and a redesigned **full-window screen-share picker**, alongside fixes for MCAS auth, SharePoint concurrent downloads, and the ozone x11 default. Most new features are opt-in via config; only the screen-share picker is on by default.

### How to enable each new feature

Add the relevant block to your `config.json`:

```json
{
  "auth": { "webauthn": { "enabled": true } },
  "multiAccount": { "enabled": true },
  "download": { "enabled": true },
  "notifications": { "timeoutType": "never" }
}
```

- **`auth.webauthn.enabled`** — FIDO2 hardware security keys. Linux only, requires the `fido2-tools` system package (`apt install fido2-tools` / `dnf install fido2-tools` / `pacman -S libfido2`). Silent no-op on macOS and Windows.
- **`multiAccount.enabled`** — multi-account profile manager (Add / Switch / rename / remove). Mutually exclusive with `auth.intune.enabled`; if both are true, multi-account is auto-disabled with a startup warning.
- **`download.enabled`** — master switch for download completion toast and taskbar progress. Once on, sub-flags `notifyOnDownloadComplete`, `showProgressBar`, and `showTitlePrefix` each default `true` and can be turned off individually.
- **`notifications.timeoutType: "never"`** — persistent notifications on Linux and Windows (workaround for GNOME and other desktops that auto-clear). Choices are `default` or `never`.

Always-on this release (no config needed): the new screen-share picker overlay, the SharePoint QUIC fix, the MCAS proxy cookie-domain fix, telemetry beacon blocking, and log-noise cleanup.

### Features

* **downloads:** drive taskbar progress bar during downloads ([#2512](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2512)) ([#2514](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2514)) ([146a736](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/146a73623dfbc3ddf6b78644ee754767afa05eb1))

* **notifications:** add notifications.timeoutType opt-in ([#2521](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2521)) ([503a5b9](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/commit/503a5b9149931cb8b272b7617bf57c2d98369fad))

## [2.9.0](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.8.1...v2.9.0) (2026-05-06)

> **Multi-account is a work-in-progress preview.** The new `multiAccount.enabled` flag (off by default) lets you keep separate Teams sessions in the same window. The Add-profile dialog and the Profiles → Switch-to menu work end-to-end in this release, but the Manage-profiles dialog, the visible top-right switcher pill, and `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts are still ahead. Useful to test, but expect rough edges and follow [#2495](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2495) for ongoing progress.


### Features

### Thanks

Big thanks to @dependabot, @jpenberthy, @MiguelAngelLV, @mvanhorn for contributing to this release.

## What's Changed in v2.8.1

### 🚀 New Features

- Add Vylen Linux to distribution packages and fix badge formatting - by @CelestifyX (#2405)
- Add Claude automation hooks for linting and PII protection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2424)
- Add trial of simili-bot for duplicate issue detection - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2438)
- Add workflow to backfill simili-bot Qdrant index - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2440)
- Add MQTT implementation plan for incoming call events - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2445) ([#2370](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2370))
- Add Wayland GPU auto-disable troubleshooting guide - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2461) ([#2410](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2410), [#2459](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2459))

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Fix: Resolve high-severity lodash-es and serialize-javascript vulnerabilities in docs-site - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2402)
- Fix call status detection when hanging up from popup window - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2406) ([#2358](https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/issues/2358))
- Update dependencies, including Electron, and resolve follow-redirects CVE - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2420)
- Fix: Unbreak cross-distro smoke test by correcting AppImage download and checks - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2422)
- Update DOMPurify to 3.4.0 for security and bug fixes (#2427)
- Fix: Pin Docusaurus v4 future flags for docs-site build compatibility - by @IsmaelMartinez (#2429)

## New Configuration Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `media.camera.resolution.enabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable camera resolution control |
| `media.camera.resolution.mode` | `string` | `"remove"` | Resolution mode: `"remove"` removes Teams' constraints allowing native camera resolution, `"override"` sets specific width/height |
| `media.camera.resolution.width` | `number` | - | Target width when mode is `"override"` |
| `media.camera.resolution.height` | `number` | - | Target height when mode is `"override"` |
| `media.camera.autoAdjustAspectRatio.enabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Fixes camera video stretching when moving Teams between monitors with different orientations |

## Deprecated Configuration Options

The following options have been moved to nested structures. The old names still work but will show deprecation warnings:

| Old Option | New Option |
|------------|------------|
| `ssoInTuneEnabled` | `auth.intune.enabled` |
| `ssoInTuneAuthUser` | `auth.intune.user` |
| `screenSharingThumbnail` | `screenSharing.thumbnail` |
| `screenLockInhibitionMethod` | `screenSharing.lockInhibitionMethod` |
| `disableAutogain` | `media.microphone.disableAutogain` |
| `videoMenu` | `media.video.menuEnabled` |

See [Configuration Reference](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/configuration) for full documentation.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.7.2...v2.7.3

## What's Changed
* Fix Intune SSO for Microsoft Identity Broker > 2.0.1 by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2069
* Upgrade yargs to v18 and update dependencies by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2097
* Fix tray icon rendering on Linux by always using nativeImage by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2096
* Add screen sharing configuration and improve hardware compatibility by  @bluvulture's and @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2068
* Release v2.7.2 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2100

New Contributors
@bluvulture made their first contribution in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2089 (that was merged with https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2068)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/compare/v2.7.1...v2.7.2

## What's Changed
* Support changelog generation for external fork PRs by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2070
* Add AppImage update info to Linux builds for third-party managers by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2067
* Fix username matching for intune auth to not be case-sensitive by @vizius-jps in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2076
* fix(ci): use unsquashfs for cross-architecture ARM AppImage extraction by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2080
* Add development roadmap and reorganize research documentation by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2079
* docs: Add GNOME Shell search provider investigation research by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2081
* Remove references to mqtt-commands-implementation research doc by @IsmaelMartinez in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2083
* chore(deps): bump tar from 6.2.1 to 7.5.7 in the npm_and_yarn group across 1 directory by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2084
* Fix: update reference to core services due to upstream change (#2077) by @bastidest in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2085
* Release v2.7.1 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/IsmaelMartinez/teams-for-linux/pull/2086

## New Contributors

### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

teams-for-linux tries to connect with someone, my webcam is running, but no preview, no streaming. It is older notebook with NVIDIA NVS 140M GPU, it has only OpenGL ES 2.0, 

### What package are you using?

deb

2. It was manual installation, through sources.list, apt-get install .deb package

openIssues.title: [Bug]: Snap 2.12.0 revision 2106 fails to launch: command.sh sources missing desktop-init.sh

labels.name: release ready
labels.description: Fix merged to main, included in the next release

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

The 2.12.0 candidate version on the snap store fails to launch with the error "/snap/teams-for-linux/2109/desktop-init.sh: No such file or directory"

### What package are you using?

snap

### Version

2.12.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Install teams-for-linux candidate version from the snap store:

$ sudo snap install --candidate teams-for-linux
teams-for-linux (candidate) 2.12.0 from Ismael Martinez Ramos (ismaelmartinez) installed
$ snap list teams-for-linux
Name             Version  Rev   Tracking          Publisher       Notes
teams-for-linux  2.12.0   2109  latest/candidate  ismaelmartinez  -

2. Launch teams-for-linux 2.12.0

teams-for-linux

3. teams-for-linux fails to start, reporting a missing shell script

$ teams-for-linux
/snap/teams-for-linux/2109/command.sh: line 2: /snap/teams-for-linux/2109/desktop-init.sh: No such file or directory
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. teams-for-linux starts normally
```

### Debug

```markdown
Output is unchanged, I don't think the program start far enough for the variables to make a difference:

$ ELECTRON_ENABLE_LOGGING=true teams-for-linux --logConfig='{"transports":{"console":{"level":"debug"}}}'
/snap/teams-for-linux/2109/command.sh: line 2: /snap/teams-for-linux/2109/desktop-init.sh: No such file or directory
```

### Additional context

Revision 2015 (2.11.1) works normally and has the file present:
```
$ ll /snap/teams-for-linux/2015/desktop-init.sh
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 1530 Jul 10  2019 /snap/teams-for-linux/2015/desktop-init.sh*
```
The corresponding file is missing in revision 2109 (2.12.0)
```
$ ll /snap/teams-for-linux/2109/desktop-init.sh
ls: cannot access '/snap/teams-for-linux/2109/desktop-init.sh': No such file or directory
```

openIssues.title: Tracking: kedzierp feature series — downloads, macOS dock/perf, attachment menu (#2645 split)
openIssues.body:
Tracking issue for the feature series @kedzierp split out of the original umbrella PR #2645. The macOS pieces are experimental, since teams-for-linux is Linux-first.

Series status:
- #2652 — download save options (`saveDirectory`, `alwaysAskWhereToSave`, `openWhenDone`), policy-block notification, device-switch guard. Merged.
- #2653 — macOS Dock presence overlay + Apple Silicon performance switches. Merged. `media.macPerformanceMode` defaults to opt-out; verified on Apple Silicon, an Intel Mac datapoint is still wanted.
- #2654 — context-menu attachment download and Save Open Document. Open, the security-sensitive piece (authenticated-session downloads, clipboard writes, hidden helper windows, SharePoint/MCAS URL rewriting), pending a focused security review.

Follow-ups:
- Intel Mac validation of the `media.macPerformanceMode` opt-out default (#2653).
- Security review of #2654 before merge.

openIssues.title: [Bug]: Frequent relogin, somtimes daily
openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

Not applicable

### Describe the bug

Misbehaving PAC proxy cause relogin

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

any recent Teams(=> 2.10.0)

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1.
2.
3.
...
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1.
2.
3.
...
```

### Debug

```markdown
ELECTRON_ENABLE_LOGGING=true teams-for-linux --logConfig='{"transports":{"console":{"level":"debug"}}}'
```

### Additional context

I think I have figured out why we see these frequent relogins. We must use a PAC proxy to access MS login and
for that we have configured --proxy-pac-url=our.proxy.com/proxy.pac

Behind our.proxy.com there are 3 different IPs in DNS for redundancy.
I have found that if ANY of those 3 server malfunction(does not answer) we see these frequent relogins.
It is especially visible when one starts Teams, then it wait for network for some 30 seconds extra.

So instead of just using one of these, Teams seems to require all of them which defeats the purpose of redundancy.

openIssues.title: [Feat]: debian package under Ubuntu Unity flavor fix icon not show in the system tray notification...
openIssues.body:
### Describe the feature

No icon present in the system tray notification with the actual launch « /usr/share/applications/teams-for-linux.desktop » file...

### Motivation

May I suggest something to add in file « **teams-for-linux.postinst** » of debian package ?

When installing under Ubuntu Unity flavor, you need to modify the « /usr/share/applications/teams-for-linux.desktop » file after installation to be able to see the icon in the status bar.

```bash
if echo $XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP | grep -i unity ; then
	sed -i 's|Exec=/opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux|Exec=env XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP="" /opt/teams-for-linux/teams-for-linux --ozone-platform=x11|g' /usr/share/applications/teams-for-linux.desktop && desktop-file-install /usr/share/applications/teams-for-linux.desktop
fi
```
Tested and working under Ubuntu Unity 22.04 & Ubuntu Unity 24.04.

Hope it help...

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

openIssues.title: [Bug]: Clicking GNOME notifications does not focus Teams or open the related conversation on Ubuntu 24.04

labels.name: release ready
labels.description: Fix merged to main, included in the next release

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

When a new message is received, Ubuntu shows a native GNOME desktop notification from Teams for Linux.  When I click this notification, nothing useful happens: - the notification disappears; - the Teams window is not restored; - the Teams window is not focused; - the related conversation is not opened.  At the same time, the Teams icon in the dock/taskbar still indicates that there is an unread message.

### What package are you using?

deb

### Version

2.10.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. Start Teams for Linux.
2. Keep Teams running in the background or minimized.
3. Receive a new message.
4. Click the native GNOME desktop notification.
5. Observe that the notification disappears, but Teams is not opened or focused.
6. The unread indicator remains visible on the Teams icon in the dock/taskbar.
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. Clicking the notification should restore the Teams window if it is minimized.
2. The existing Teams window should be focused.
3. Ideally, the conversation related to the notification should be opened.
4. At minimum, the Teams window should be brought to the foreground.
```

### Debug

```markdown
ELECTRON_ENABLE_LOGGING=true teams-for-linux --logConfig='{"transports":{"console":{"level":"debug"}}}'
```

### Additional context

Environment:

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat)
- Desktop Environment: GNOME 46.0
- Session Type: X11
- Package: teams-for-linux Debian package
- Executable: /usr/bin/teams-for-linux

Installed package information:

teams-for-linux:
  Installed: 2.10.0
  Candidate: 2.10.0
  Version table:
 *** 2.10.0 500
        500 https://repo.teamsforlinux.de/debian stable/main amd64 Packages
        100 /var/lib/dpkg/status

The issue is reproducible consistently on my system.

Even if opening the exact conversation is limited by Microsoft Teams Web, clicking the notification should at least restore or focus the existing Teams for Linux window.

_No response_

### Additional context

Some credential providers for NSS are password protected, particularly Smartcards accessed through PKCS#11 (configured as a PKCS#11 module, typically under /etc/pkcs11/modules/ or in a nssdb folder). In that case, NSS cannot access the certificates stored on the Smartcard without unlocking it with a PIN before. The Chromium framework implements native "PIN dialogs" for Windows and MacOS, but as there is no "native" UI library under Linux, it uses a callback for user-implemented dialogs under Linux. 

For a long time, Electron missed an API for this feature, but with Electron 33, an additional method was added to the app class to allow this: 

https://www.electronjs.org/blog/electron-33-0#notable-changes
https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/41205

Users of this API set the handler to a custom dialog callback that asks the User for a PIN and returns it. Afterwards, the app needs to listen to the 'select-client-certificate' event to potentially present and let the user choose the correct certificate to use for authentication.

Here is a gist with an example implementation* that can just be dropped in an example Electron projects main file and will open the certificate test page https://prod.idrix.eu/secure/ which displays information about the certificate used for authentication: https://gist.github.com/agemuend/8fd859ee074517188744f8c6fc11c9a4

*AI has been used for the example implementation as I'm usually not a nodejs developer (I'm more at home with C++). Thats also the reason why I didn't directly create a PR. I fear it would probably not be very useful.

openIssues.title: [Feat]: FIDO2 missing UI prompt when waiting for authenticator touch (user presence check)

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
**Describe the bug**

After entering the FIDO2 PIN, the YubiKey starts blinking to request a physical touch (the WebAuthn "user presence" check). At this point, teams-for-linux shows no feedback whatsoever -- the UI is silent. An inexperienced user has no way of knowing they need to touch the key, and will typically assume authentication failed or the app is broken.

The stock Microsoft Teams client handles this correctly: it displays a small dialog prompting the user to touch their security key.

**To reproduce**

1. Configure FIDO2/WebAuthn login with a hardware key (e.g. YubiKey).
2. Initiate sign-in and enter the PIN when prompted.
3. Observe: YubiKey LED starts blinking, waiting for touch. No UI feedback appears in teams-for-linux.

**Expected behavior**

A visible prompt (dialog, overlay, or notification) should appear after PIN entry, instructing the user to touch their hardware key. This should remain visible until the touch is registered or the operation times out.

**Actual behavior**

No UI feedback. The app silently awaits the touch with no indication to the user.

**Environment**

- teams-for-linux version: 2.10.0
- Hardware key: YubiKey (Yubico OTP+FIDO+CCID)
- OS: Linux

**Additional context**

This is the final step in the FIDO2 flow introduced in PR #2357. The PIN prompt is already handled correctly; only the subsequent user presence prompt is missing. Closing this gap would make the full FIDO2 experience functional for non-technical users.

openIssues.title: feat: Register as 1Password trusted browser for autofill support

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
## Summary

When a Teams session expires and the Microsoft login page appears, users with 1Password for Linux cannot use the global autofill shortcut (default: Ctrl+Shift+X) because `teams-for-linux` is not registered as a trusted browser.

## Background

1Password for Linux maintains a browser allowlist at `/etc/1password/custom_allowed_browsers`. Applications listed there can receive credentials via the 1Password browser extension's native messaging protocol. The file ships with common browsers commented out as examples, and custom entries can be added — one executable name per line.

Since `teams-for-linux` is an Electron app that regularly presents the Microsoft login page (session expiry, reauthentication), registering it as a trusted browser would enable seamless autofill of credentials and TOTP codes.

## Proposed Solution

Add package-level install/remove hooks that manage the `custom_allowed_browsers` entry:

| Format | Mechanism |
|--------|-----------|
| **deb** | `afterInstall` / `afterRemove` electron-builder hooks |
| **rpm** | `fpm --after-install` / `--before-remove` passthrough |
| **snap** | `snap.hooks` (install/remove) via electron-builder; requires `system-files` interface for `/etc/1password/` access |
| **AppImage / tar.gz** | Standalone `build/1password-setup.sh` script users run once with sudo |

### Key properties

- **No app code changes** — purely packaging scripts
- **Idempotent** — safe to run multiple times
- **Guarded** — only acts when 1Password is installed (`/etc/1password/custom_allowed_browsers` exists)
- **Clean uninstall** — entry is removed on package removal
- **Graceful degradation** — snap hook exits silently if `system-files` interface is not connected

## Process Name

All package formats (deb, rpm, AppImage, snap, tar.gz) produce the same process name `teams-for-linux` (confirmed from electron-builder's `executableName` config and AppImage AppRun), so a single allowlist entry covers all variants.

## References

- [1Password: Connect your browser (Linux)](https://support.1password.com/cs/connect-1password-x-linux/)
- Precedent for snap `system-files` approval: `chromium:etc-chromium-browser-policies`

No

### Describe the bug

Settings and login is not preserved

### What package are you using?

deb

openIssues.title: Tracking: migrate snap base from core22 to core24

labels.name: blocked
labels.description: This issue depends on external projects

openIssues.body:
### Background

Our snap currently ships `base: core22`. While validating the removal of the forced `--ozone-platform=x11` default (#2506, tracking #2508), testing on newer distros surfaced a separate problem: on Ubuntu 26.04 the snap selects native Wayland correctly and PipeWire screen-share detection fires, but then segfaults with a `MESA-LOADER iris/kms_swrast` error (see the #2551 / #2508 reports). This looks like the core22 runtime's bundled Mesa being too old relative to a 26.04 host, which is exacerbated once the GBM / native-Wayland path is taken. It is independent of the ozone flag: removing the flag uncovers it rather than causing it.

Moving the snap to `base: core24` (Ubuntu 24.04 / Noble runtime, with a newer Mesa stack) is the candidate fix for that class of crash.

### History (why we are not on core24 already)

core24 was tried once and reverted:

- #1884 moved `base` `core22` -> `core24` to fix a `LIBDBUS_PRIVATE_1.12.20` startup crash (#1881).
- #1891 reverted it the same day, back to `core22`, **entangled with an Electron downgrade to 37.7.0** (v2.6.3).

The reason core24 failed then was a tooling failure, not a core24 runtime problem: electron-builder did not properly support core24, the generated snap was malformed, and the binary segfaulted on startup (`libpcre.so.3` missing). See electron-builder issue [electron-userland/electron-builder#8548](https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/issues/8548).

### What changed (the unblock)

electron-builder added beta core24 support in [electron-userland/electron-builder#9517](https://github.com/electron-userland/electron-builder/pull/9517), **merged 2026-05-27**. It is not in the current stable release (26.11.1); it is slated to land in `app-builder-lib@26.12.0` as beta. We are currently on `electron-builder@^26.8.1`, which is too old.

So this work is **blocked on electron-builder 26.12.0** reaching at least an installable beta tag, ideally stable.

### What the migration involves (not a one-line base flip)

Per the electron-builder core24 PR, core24 is a config restructure, not just a `base` value change:

- Bump electron-builder to `26.12.0+`.
- Use the new `snapcraft` config key (the legacy `snap` key only supports core18/20/22) with `base: core24`.
- The `gnome` extension becomes the default and is required for GPU / theming. This is most likely the mechanism that fixes the Mesa segfault.
- `forceX11: true` replaces the old `executableArgs: ["--ozone-platform=x11"]` approach (note the inverted semantics, and that #2506 is separately removing that forced flag, so the two need to be reconciled).
- The Snap Store publish flow was rewritten to call `snapcraft` directly with `SNAPCRAFT_STORE_CREDENTIALS` (`snapcraft export-login`), so the CI publish secret/flow will likely need updating.
- The `plugs` array may differ per base and is not fully settled upstream; verify against an actual edge build.

### Validation plan — full cross-distro test, not smoke

The automated cross-distro smoke test only checks that the app launches and renders. core24 changes the snap runtime and the GPU/Mesa path, so this needs the **full authenticated cross-distro test** (`tests/cross-distro/`):

1. `./run.sh ubuntu wayland --login` (log in once via noVNC, session persists), then
2. `./run.sh ubuntu wayland --test` (or `./run-all-tests.sh` across all configurations) to exercise the authenticated flows.
3. Manual screen-share, camera, and call checks on a real Wayland session, since those are what the runtime change is most likely to affect.

Important: the in-repo harness uses Ubuntu 24.04, so it will not reproduce the Ubuntu 26.04 Mesa segfault directly. The 26.04 crash needs validation on a real 26.04 host (or by adding a 26.04 image to the harness), separate from the login-first run.

Validate on the snap `edge` channel before promoting to stable.

### Staging

This is the third of three deliberately-separated changes, each shipping in its own release so their regressions stay isolated: ozone-default removal (#2506), Electron 41 -> 42 (#2589), and this snap core24 migration.

### Tracking checklist

- [ ] electron-builder 26.12.0 available (beta tag or stable)
- [ ] Reconcile `forceX11` vs the #2506 ozone-default removal
- [ ] Migrate `build.snap` to the new `snapcraft` / core24 config + gnome extension

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Detect scheduled-meeting-start for MQTT — Phase 2 of #2370
openIssues.body:
## Background

This is a follow-up to issue #2370, splitting out its Phase 2 so the original thread stays as the incoming-call (Phase 1) record. Phase 1 shipped in PR #2572: the {topicPrefix}/incoming-call topic publishes "true" on ring and "false" on end for 1:1 ring-type calls, giving MQTT parity with incomingCallCommand. That covers Scenario 1. Phase 2 is what andyrozman asked for: detect when a SCHEDULED meeting actually becomes active and publish it to MQTT, so Home Assistant automations can fire when he is away from the screen.

## The hard part

Teams only fires the "<name> started the meeting" notification when someone explicitly clicks Start. Earlier digs into Teams internals did not find a reliable event for this, and our previous tests may simply not have triggered the notification often enough to judge. A spike is needed before committing to a detection method.

## Two paths to spike

Path A (notification/DOM/React-store detection) watches for the actual "started the meeting" toast. This is fragile because the wording can change without notice and it can false-positive (a chat message mentioning a meeting, other toast text). Path B (calendar polling, maintainer's idea) queries the calendar every ~15 minutes, then a few seconds before a scheduled meeting's start time checks whether the meeting is still on the calendar. If it is, assume it is active and emit a "meeting starting" event. Note this is "meeting starting" (inferred from the schedule), not "meeting started" (which only happens on the explicit Start click). It may emit even when nobody actually joins. andyrozman has said a best-effort signal with occasional false positives would still be more useful than nothing.

## Scope

(1) spike first to confirm whether Path A or Path B is reliable enough, (2) if a good-enough signal exists, publish a new topic (name TBD, e.g. {topicPrefix}/meeting-starting) plus Home Assistant auto-discovery, documenting the caveat, (3) if neither is reliable, document the limitation and close.

## Not covered by #2465

That issue tracks media-state sensors (mute, camera, screen-sharing, microphone), all shipped. This meeting-start detection is separate and tracked here.

openIssues.title: [Feat]: Multi-account profile switcher (ADR-020 tracking)
openIssues.body:
## Summary

Tracking issue for the multi-account profile switcher work led by @jpenberthy, designed in [ADR-020](https://ismaelmartinez.github.io/teams-for-linux/development/adr/020-multi-account-profile-switcher/). Several PRs have already shipped to main and will land in 2.9.0; the rest of Phase 1 plus Phases 2 and 3 are ahead. This single issue is where to follow progress and coordinate; please do not refile separate tickets for the same feature, comment here instead.

The design is settled in ADR-020, this issue only tracks delivery.

## Status

### Phase 1: MVP

Per-profile `WebContentsView`s, top-right switcher overlay, `Profiles` menu with Add / Switch / Manage flows, `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts, first-run Profile 0 migration, the six Phase 1 `profile-*` IPC channels, plus the audit-table shared-state migrations.

- [x] ADR-020 published (#2426)
- [x] `multiAccount.enabled` flag and Intune mutual-exclusion check (#2450)
- [x] `ProfilesManager` scaffold and login state migration (#2478)
- [x] Per-profile `WebContentsView` lifecycle + Profile 0 bootstrap (#2483)
- [x] `Profiles` submenu with Switch-to (#2489)
- [x] Add-profile dialog with first-run bootstrap fix (#2496)
- [x] Manage-profiles dialog (#2510)
- [ ] Top-right switcher chrome overlay (the visible pill ADR-020 commits to)
- [ ] `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts for pinned profiles
- [x] Screen-preview partition derived from active profile (audit row 2) — retired as not needed; preview no longer carries a session (#2534), confirmed in #2626 (2026-06-07)
- [x] `CustomBackground` per-instance refactor (audit row 3) — Phase 1 shared-state field move done in #2626 (2026-06-07); per-profile instantiation deferred to the per-view work
- [x] Byte-identity-when-disabled E2E smoke test (covered by `multi-account-disabled.spec.js`)

#### Phase 1 polish discovered post-MVP

- [x] Screen-share picker handler not bound to profile partition sessions (#2529)

### Phase 2: Background notifications

Per-partition preload notification shim and unread-count tagging, aggregated tray badge, per-profile unread dots, `disableNotifications` and `muted` plumbing per profile.

- [ ] Per-partition preload notification shim
- [ ] Unread-count tagging
- [ ] Aggregated tray badge
- [ ] Per-profile unread dots
- [ ] `disableNotifications` and `muted` plumbing per profile

### Phase 3: Power features

`--profile-id` CLI flag end-to-end, keyboard shortcut to cycle profiles, pinned-profile sidebar (max 5), drag-to-reorder.

- [ ] `--profile-id` CLI flag end-to-end
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut to cycle profiles
- [ ] Pinned-profile sidebar (max 5)
- [ ] Drag-to-reorder

## Where to test

Set `"multiAccount": { "enabled": true }` in your config to opt in. With #2496 merged, the **Profiles → Add profile…** menu entry is wired up end-to-end, so you can add and switch between profiles from the UI. The Manage dialog is ready on `feat/multi-account-manage-dialog` and will land in the next PR; the top-right switcher pill and the `Ctrl+Shift+1…5` shortcuts are still ahead.

## Context

Originally requested in #72, #1656, #1830, #438, and tangentially via #1984 (which was redirected to ADR-010's Quick Chat as the alternative for that specific framing). Those tickets stay closed; subscribers will be redirected here for ongoing progress.

@jpenberthy this is the place to drop status updates, ETAs, and any "stuck on X" notes so the community can follow along and offer to take individual pieces. Thanks again for driving this.

labels.name: awaiting user feedback
labels.description: Waiting for user to confirm they still need this feature

openIssues.body:
### Can you reproduce this bug on the Microsoft Teams web app (https://teams.microsoft.com)?

No

### Describe the bug

the camera turns off mid call at random and cannot be turned back on

### What package are you using?

flatpak

### Version

2.8.0

### Reproduction steps

```markdown
1. start the app
2. join a video call
3. after an undefined time camera turns off and refuses to boot back up in app
```

### Expected Behavior

```markdown
1. start app
2. join a video call
3. the call is uninterrupted by camera issues due to the software
```

### Debug

```markdown
unfortunately I cannot provide as I didn't have it running during the meeting and I use the app very infrequently
```

### Additional context

To make sure this is clear, the camera itself is perfectly fine, other apps were able to use it no problem even during the call(AFTER the bug was made present, to confirm it was the app itself having issues). It's a NEXIGO HD webcam

openIssues.title: Track: graduate the cross-repo PROPOSE soak (G10) — review ~2026-07-23 after a month of dry-run
openIssues.body:
## Context
This is a tracking issue for the G10 graduation decision within the cross-repo PROPOSE program. The full machinery for goals G1 through G9 is shipped and merged to main, with G9 (the cross-repo issue format) merged as PR #304. The G10 observation soak was started by PR #306, merged on 2026-06-23, which added the propose phase to the weekly-ideate.yml workflow.

## What is running now
Every Monday at 06:00 UTC, the weekly-ideate workflow runs observe,ideate,propose in dry-run. PROPOSE files no issues; it only emits the soak log: "DRY RUN — would create issue ..." lines recording each idea's resolved destination, and which cross-repo nudges would route to another repo versus fall back to the host backlog ("[cross-repo -> X]" vs "[target: X -> host (reason)]"). The cross-repo allow-lists propose-targets and propose-classes in .github/roadmap.yml stay EMPTY, so nothing crosses and no issues are filed. The only write in dry-run is the idempotent host-label ensure, which is a no-op because the labels already exist.

## When to revisit
The decision to make after a clean month-long soak targets a review around 2026-07-23, when back from holidays.

## The graduation decision
Review the Monday weekly-ideate workflow run logs across the soak period and confirm the cross-repo candidate flow is sane: which repos surface as candidates, on what statistic-bearing finding (standards-gap, policy-drift, tier-uplift), and that host fallbacks have sensible reasons. Then graduate exactly ONE class live via a SEPARATE reviewed PR. tier-uplift is the recommended first class (its body is a deterministic list of named failing checks and it already names its repo). Graduation = three deliberate, separately-reviewed flips: (1) populate propose-targets with one onboarded, eligible target repo AND propose-classes with tier-uplift: true; (2) set require_approval: false; (3) set INPUT_DRY_RUN: false for the relevant run. Never autonomous, never auto-merged, and no cron is added (ADR-011 keeps where-it-writes independent of when-it-runs). Broaden one class per subsequent reviewed config PR. A target repo must be onboarded (carry the repo-butler marker in its CLAUDE.md) and eligible (not archived, fork, or excluded) to receive a cross-repo issue.

## How to review the soak
Read the "Repo Butler Weekly Ideate" workflow run logs on GitHub Actions for the dry-run lines above, or query the MCP tool get_governance_findings for live tier-uplift findings to anticipate candidates.

## Out of scope
G11 (the optional net-new deterministic classes description-gap and topics-gap) stays parked behind G10. PROPOSE-on-a-schedule for cross-repo writes is a separate decision and shares the same soak gate.

## References
ADR-010 (docs/decisions/010-cross-repo-proposal-destinations.md), ADR-011 (docs/decisions/011-portfolio-informed-generic-proposals.md), the "Cross-repo PROPOSE destinations" section of ROADMAP.md, PR #304 (G9), PR #306 (soak start).

openIssues.title: MLX backend: per-request RNG / seed is non-functional (mlx_lm.server 0.31.3) — upstream ml-explore/mlx-lm#1245
openIssues.body:
## Summary

The MLX backend (`mlx_lm.server` 0.31.3, the current latest) has a **non-functional per-request RNG**: at any temperature it returns byte-identical output for the same prompt, and the OpenAI-compatible `seed` parameter is silently ignored (different seeds → identical output). This is an **upstream bug**, tracked at **ml-explore/mlx-lm#1245** ("mlx_lm.server: seed parameter silently ignored", open since 2026-05-05, no fix yet). This issue records our independent reproduction and the consequences for delegate-local's experiments.

## Independent reproduction (this host, 35B, 2026-06-19)

- Temperature **is** honoured: temp 1.0 produced a different sentence than temp 0.0 (so the server is not clamping to greedy — our temp-0 default is client-side, `delegate.sh:202`, and overridable via `DELEGATE_TEMPERATURE`).
- Four identical temp-1.0 requests → **byte-identical**.
- Explicit `seed` 1 / 2 / 3 / 42 at temp 1.0 → **all byte-identical** (and merely including a seed degraded the 35B into a degenerate repeating loop). `server.py:956-957` does call `mx.random.seed(args.seed)` per request, but it has no effect on the output.
- **Control:** the same model `qwen3.6:35b-a3b` on **Ollama** at temp 1.0 varies correctly across seeds 1/2/3 (three distinct coherent outputs, one matching the MLX greedy result). So the determinism is a property of *this server build's RNG*, not of the weights or MLX-the-framework.

## Impact on delegate-local

1. **Any MLX experiment that depends on sampling diversity is invalid.** In particular the fan-out / self-consistency result (PR #317, ADR 0018) was measured on MLX, which means it tested *N copies of one sample*, not fan-out. It should be re-run on `DELEGATE_BACKEND=ollama` before its conclusion is trusted.
2. **MLX greedy reps add no variance** (stdev=0 by construction) — fine for the baseline matrix, but reps must not be read as a stochastic-stability estimate.
3. Re-running the fan-out test on Ollama (where diversity is real) still shows **no lift on our fixtures**: the routed instruct models are at single-shot ceiling (T5 = 6/6 on gemma4 / qwen3-coder-next / qwen3.5-27b) and the closed-form tasks are low-entropy (1–2 distinct outputs across 5 temp-1.0 seeds). So fan-out remains a poor fit for this skill's task class — but for *task-structural* reasons (no single-shot headroom, no discrete vote function for free-text), with the MLX-determinism confound now removed and quantified.

## Decision / scope

- Treat the **MLX backend as deterministic-per-request** until upstream #1245 is fixed; use **Ollama** for any diversity-dependent measurement.
- Fan-out is **not** delegate-local's quality lever regardless (see #317 + this issue).
- Optional upstream contribution: corroborate on ml-explore/mlx-lm#1245 and/or investigate the root cause (it is downstream of the `mx.random.seed` call but upstream of the actual sampling, per the reporter's own bisection). Tracked separately if pursued.

Upstream: ml-explore/mlx-lm#1245

releases.name: v0.1.0 — Pre-election release
releases.body:
First tagged release of VoteScot ahead of the 7 May 2026 Scottish Parliament election.

Covers all 73 constituencies with 434 candidates across 7 major parties. Features include the vote compass quiz, interactive constituency map with postcode lookup, polling trends, constituency projections with methodology transparency, candidate profiles with track records, and a side-by-side comparison view.

meta.name: votescot
meta.description: Open-source vote compass for Scottish Parliament elections

releases.name: v1.0.0
releases.body:
WiFi Sentinel v1.0.0 — multi-persona WiFi and network security analyser.

Features:
- CLI scanner with 10 scanner modules and three-tier tool fallback
- Five AI-style persona agents (red team, blue team, compliance, net engineer, privacy)
- Compliance scoring against CIS Wireless, NIST 800-153, IEEE 802.11, OWASP IoT
- WiFi RF intelligence: channel maps, saturation scoring, rogue AP detection
- External reconnaissance: DNS, TLS, headers, WHOIS, cert transparency
- Next.js dashboard with scan history, trends, persona perspectives
- HTML report export from CLI and dashboard
- Scan persistence with history, trend, and diff commands
- Scheduled scanning via launchd/cron
- Local OUI database (no external API calls)
- Linux support
- 44 unit tests, ESLint, GitHub Actions CI

meta.name: wifisentinel
meta.description: WiFi security scanner with multi-persona analysis, compliance scoring, and RF intelligence

releases.name: v0.1.0 — Repository Strategist
releases.body:
First release of the Repository Strategist — a GitHub App that maintains institutional memory for software projects and uses it to provide doc-grounded triage and strategic intelligence.

## Features

- Multi-phase triage pipeline: deterministic template parsing (Phase 1), vector search + Gemini suggestions (Phase 2), enhancement context briefs (Phase 4a)
- Synthesis engine: weekly cluster detection, ADR drift detection, upstream impact analysis, posted as shadow repo briefings
- Enhancement research agent: mirror issues in shadow repos, context briefs, full research synthesis with safety gates
- MCP server: four tools exposing triage, synthesis, health, and trend data to Claude Code agents
- Event journal: records all repo activity for temporal analysis
- Auto-ingest: re-embeds changed documentation on push
- Per-repo config via `.github/butler.json`
- Live dashboard at `/dashboard`
- Two-layer safety: structural validator + LLM reviewer on all outputs
- Shadow repo approval gates: `lgtm`/`reject` for triage, `research`/`use as context`/`reject` for enhancements

## Infrastructure

Go 1.26.1 on Google Cloud Run, Neon PostgreSQL with pgvector, Gemini 2.5 Flash, GitHub Actions CI/CD with Workload Identity Federation.

## Documentation

- 13 Architecture Decision Records in `docs/adr/`
- Roadmap with six streams in `docs/plans/2026-03-04-roadmap.md`
- Security policy in `SECURITY.md`

meta.name: github-issue-triage-bot
meta.description: Standalone Go service that triages GitHub issues using vector search and LLM analysis

releases.name: v0.5.0
releases.body:
### Minor Changes

-   fdb6e2d: Support deriving domains from Compass labels or custom fields. The `domain` option now accepts a mapping config (`{ from: 'label' | 'customField', key?, mapping, fallback? }`) in addition to the existing static `{ id, name, version }` form. Services resolve to domains per-component; each mapped domain is versioned independently and created lazily on first use.

releases.name: v0.4.0
releases.body:
### Minor Changes

-   cf75f82: Enrich team entities with description, avatar, and members from the Compass Teams v2 API. Add incremental mode (`incremental: true`) that skips unchanged services using SHA-256 content hashing.

releases.name: v0.3.1
releases.body:
# @ismaelmartinez/generator-atlassian-compass-event-catalog

## 0.3.0

### Minor Changes

-   feat: presentation layer improvements
    -   Populate `service.attachments` from Compass links via structured links (url, title, type, icon)
    -   Categorise links into subsections: Compass, Development, Operations, Documentation, Other
    -   Expose `StructuredLink` type and pass it as optional third parameter to custom markdown templates
    -   Surface `customFields` as a markdown table between Links and Dependencies sections
    -   Map Compass `typeId` to `service.styles.icon` using Lucide icon names

## 0.2.1

### Patch Changes

-   53a5fd2: fix: address security vulnerabilities in token logging, path traversal, and XSS
    -   Prevent potential API token leakage via error logs in team fetch failure handling
    -   Sanitize local spec file paths to reject path traversal sequences (`../`) and absolute paths
    -   Sanitize custom field text values from Compass API to prevent XSS in rendered markdown

## 0.2.0

### Minor Changes

-   a547fd4: feat: map Compass DEPENDS_ON relationships to service markdown
    -   Parse `relationships.DEPENDS_ON` from Compass YAML and resolve referenced services
    -   Add a "Dependencies" section to generated service markdown with links to dependent services
    -   Use a two-pass approach: first collect all services, then resolve dependencies between them
    -   Handle missing dependency targets gracefully (log warning, don't crash)
    -   Services without dependencies show "No known dependencies."

## 0.1.0

### Minor Changes

-   d3f5ee0: Modernize and fix core gaps (Phase 1)
    -   Replace local Service type with SDK's Service type for richer metadata support
    -   Map Compass lifecycle, tier, and labels to EventCatalog badges
    -   Map Compass repository links to service repository URL
    -   Map Compass ownerId to service owners
    -   Support updating existing services with overrideExisting option (defaults to true)
    -   Add Zod validation for generator configuration with clear error messages

-   26590b0: Support all Compass component types (Phase 2)
    -   Remove SERVICE-only restriction — all Compass types (APPLICATION, LIBRARY, CAPABILITY, CLOUD_RESOURCE, DATA_PIPELINE, MACHINE_LEARNING_MODEL, OTHER, UI_ELEMENT, WEBSITE) are now processed as EventCatalog services
    -   Add `typeFilter` option to selectively process only specified component types
    -   Add component type badge showing the Compass type (e.g. APPLICATION, LIBRARY) on each service

## 0.0.9

### Patch Changes

-   e6e0f5c: Phase 1: Update dependencies to latest versions

    Updated dependencies to latest versions (Phase 1 of modernization plan):

    -   @eventcatalog/sdk: ^2.0.0 → ^2.9.0 (9 minor versions, no breaking changes)
    -   @changesets/cli: ^2.28.1 → ^2.29.7
    -   chalk: ^5.4.1 → ^5.6.2

    All tests pass with updated dependencies. See UPDATE_PLAN.md for full modernization roadmap.

## 0.0.8

### Patch Changes

-   3c0a302: Updating depencencies and moving to eventcatalog/sdk 2.0.0

## 0.0.7

### Patch Changes

-   06e4c92: Fix docs and update the dependencies

## 0.0.6

### Patch Changes

-   5600c7a: fix chalk version to 4 to avoid ESM breaking changes

## 0.0.5

### Patch Changes

-   7bfa0ac: update dependencies and adding default PROJECT_DIR when not present

## 0.0.4

### Patch Changes

-   85cf3f5: update dependencies and improve documentation

## 0.0.3

### Patch Changes

-   8694a60: Fixing #9 by moving away from Tiles as they don't support external links on bundle objects

## 0.0.2

### Patch Changes

-   92170b3: Initial BETA release

    -   Sanitize custom field text values from Compass API to prevent XSS in rendered markdown

releases.name: v1.1.0 — Curated Channels
releases.body:
## What's new

- 44 hand-picked channels (UK news, entertainment, movies, music, sports, kids, docs) as the default experience
- Automated channel validation script testing streams from iptv-org playlists
- Weekly GitHub Action keeps validation data fresh
- 1,800+ validated channels available via Find > Validated for discovery
- Split export buttons (All / Favs) to prevent accidental favourites-only export
- Wrapping category filter pills instead of horizontal scroll
- New channels highlight in sidebar when added from Find panel
- Community health files (code of conduct, issue/PR templates)

## Breaking changes

None — the app loads picks.json as the default channel list, falling back to the built-in list if offline.
